特記事項: 【合併】総合日本学習科目（Japanese Economy and Business)（CIEC）/Japanese Economy and Business（国際学部）
授業方法: Seminar. Based on daily readings and class discussions, students will gain an understanding of characteristics and phenomena of the Japanese economy and Japanese business. By discussing real world cases of business success and failure in Japan and practicing cross-cultural communication techniques, students will also develop a portfolio of best practices in business communication and management.

第2回: Japan’s Development after World War II
Reading of assigned reading and preparation for class discussion.
第3回: Natural Disasters in Japan and its Impact on the Economy
Reading of assigned reading and preparation for class discussion.
第4回: Project-Based-Learning (PBL) class with Japanese students which requires tasks to be completed outside of class hours.
第5回: Preparation for Field Trip to Hiroshima
第8回: Human Resource Management in Japanese Companies
Reading of assigned reading and preparation for class discussion.
第9回: The Recruitment Process in the Japanese Firm
Reading of assigned reading and preparation for class discussion.
第10回: Project-Based-Learning (PBL) class with Japanese students which requires tasks to be completed outside of class hours.
第11回: Globalization and the Rise of Flexible Labor
Reading of assigned reading and preparation for class discussion.
第12回: Women and Foreigners in the Japanese Labor Market
Reading of assigned reading and preparation for class discussion.
第13回: Gender Roles, Workplace Harassment and Mental Health
Reading of assigned reading and preparation for class discussion.
第14回: Project-Based-Learning (PBL) class with Japanese students which requires tasks to be completed outside of class hours.
第15回: Preparation for Field Trip to Matsuyama
第18回: Doing Business in Japan: Consumer Behavior, Market Entry and Marketing 1
Reading of assigned reading and preparation for class discussion.
第19回: Doing Business in Japan: Consumer Behavior, Market Entry and Marketing 2
Reading of assigned reading and preparation for class discussion.
第20回: Project-Based-Learning (PBL) class with Japanese students which requires tasks to be completed outside of class hours.
第21回: Japanese Negotiation Styles and Decision-Making Process
Reading of assigned reading and preparation for class discussion.
第22回: Different Working Styles in the US and Japan, Summary and Wrap-up
Reading of assigned reading and preparation for class discussion.
トピック.授業外学習2: Besides the study required outside class as specified above, students will also need time to present one of the class topics in the form of an activity and write a brief field trip report.

教科書/Required texts0: A reading list of relevant articles will be provided in the first class.
参考文献・資料/Reference books0: Tim Craig & Matthias Hennings (2022). Competing in the Japanese Market: A Case Study Approach
学位授与の方針との関連Relationship to Diploma Polic:
各授業科目は、各学部・研究科の定めるディプロマ・ポリシー（DP）・カリキュラム・ポリシー（CP）に基づき、カリキュラム上に配置されています。
DP・CPを意識して本授業科目の学修を進めることで、各学部・研究科の期待する能力が養成されます。
各学部・研究科のDP、CPや教育課程表（授業科目一覧等）は、下記リンクこちらに掲載していますので学修計画の参考にしてください。

成績評価Grading0: 評価基準等　Grading Criteria etc.
成績評価Grading2: 備考　Note Class Participation 50%: Active participation in class discussions, including assigned homework such as readings / short presentations, and PBL classes with Japanese students which require tasks to be completed outside of class hours.Field Trip Report (50%): Academic field trip report on one of the field trips during the summer school (800-1200 words).
備考Note0: This course is part of the KGU Summer School and also includes overnight field trips. Please check the summer intensive schedule for details. If you want to contact the instructor before the course you can email to: matthias.hennings@kwansei.ac.jpThe course is taught Tuesdays 2nd & 3rd period, Wednesdays 2nd & 3rd period and Thursdays 2nd period. Class Room: Tue (MR 201), Wed (MR 201 in 2nd and G 208 in 3rd period), Thu (G 105).

name: 【330】Japanese Economy and Business／Japanese Economy and Business [EN]
